## Structure

TCP S→C 0x8318 — likely a **team/party-related event**. Fixed
7-byte body. Verified 2026-05-10 against both retail samples
(2/17 captures — only the two PvP-party captures
LONG_PARTY_A/B emit it).

```
[0..1]   83 18                  TCP opcode (constant)
[2]      0x01                   CONSTANT (event-type / status?)
[3..6]   target_uid LE32        target character UID
                                 (0x000184a5 in both samples)
```

Sample retail bytes:
```
83 18 01 a5 84 01 00     target_uid = 0x000184a5 (teammate)
83 18 01 a5 84 01 00     identical — same teammate
```

## Variants

Single 7-byte form. The byte-identical content across two
captures (different sessions, same teammate UID) confirms this
is per-target_uid data, not per-session.

## Observed contexts

Only emitted in the two party-PvP captures
(`LONG_PARTY_A/B` × 1 each). Both captures share an
overlapping team composition, hence the identical target_uid.

## Open questions

- The 0x18 sub-byte is in the 0x83XX TCP "team / chat" family
  alongside 0x8317 (Chat) and 0x8388 (TeamEvent per
  catalog_extract names).
- Possibly a "team-member online status" notification — the
  static target_uid suggests it's identifying a specific
  party member.
- Could also be a friend-list or contact-update event.

## Server-side handler

**NOT IMPLEMENTED** in Ceres-J. No `case 0x8318` handler in
the TCP dispatcher. Bytes silently consumed.

For full retail parity (low priority — only 2 retail samples,
party-only):
1. Recognise 0x8318 in the TCP dispatcher.
2. Investigate trigger semantics by replicating party
   formation in a test session.
